zh.lifeSummary: 當Aline De St. Paul於大約1354年出生時，她的父親Stephen de Mallory46歲，母親Margaret Revell36歲。她於1364年與William De Cheywynd II結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1411年在英國英格蘭史洛普爾謝特德享年59歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Aline De St. Paul于大约1354年出生时，她的父亲Stephen de Mallory46岁，母亲Margaret Revell36岁。她于1364年与William De Cheywynd II结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于大约1411年在英国英格兰史洛普爾謝特德享年59岁。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: St.
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized spelling of French   St  .   Denis  .

variants.name: St
meanings: Americanized spelling of French   St  .   Denis  .

variants.name: Denis
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Camille, Jacques, Armand, Marcel, Andre, Pierre, Serge, Yvan, Alphonse, Edouard, Francois, Gaetane.  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Jorge, Roberto, Angel, Francisco, Joaquin, Juan, Manuel, Miguel, Ramon, Rolando.

French, Walloon, Breton, West Indian (mainly Haiti), German, Dutch, English, and Portuguese; Spanish and Galician (Denís): from the personal name Denis, Spanish and Galician Denís, from Latin Dionysius ‘(follower) of Dionysos’ (see Dennis ). Compare Dennee .
Ukrainian and Rusyn: variant transliteration of   Denys  , a cognate of 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Paul
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, French, German, Dutch, Swedish, West Indian (mainly Haiti, also e.g. Saint Lucia), and African (mainly Nigeria and Tanzania): from the personal name Paul (from Latin Paulus ‘small’), which has always been popular in Christendom. It was the name adopted by the Pharisee Saul of Tarsus after his conversion to Christianity on the road to Damascus in about AD 34. He was a most energetic missionary to the Gentiles in the Roman Empire, and played a very significant role in establishing Christianity as a major world religion. The name was borne also by numerous other early Christian saints. It is also occasionally borne by Jews; the reasons for this are not clear. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages and their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Greek Pavlis , Slovenian Pavel and Pavlič (see Pavlic ), Polish Paweł (see Pawel ) and Pawlicki , Assyrian/Chaldean Polous and Polus . In France, this surname is most common in Brittany (see 2 below).
Breton (mainly Finistère): from a Frenchified form of the personal name Paol, Breton form of Paul .
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Phóil ‘son of Paul’. Compare McFall .

variants.name: Pavlis
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Greek Despina, Nikolaos, Vasilios.
Greek: from a derivative of the personal name Pavlos (see Paul ), or a shortened form of any of various patronymics derived from this name. Compare Paules and Paulis .
Czech and Slovak (also Pavliš); Croatian (Pavliš): derivative of the personal name Pavel (Slovak commonly Pavol, Croatian commonly Pavao), an equivalent of Paul . Compare Paulis .

variants.name: Pablo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Pedro, Alfredo, Cesar, Jaime, Manuel, Vicente, Amado, Arturo, Corazon, Felipe, Jose, Juan.
Spanish: from the personal name Pablo, an equivalent of Paul .

variants.name: Pahl
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Claus, Erwin, Fritz, Goetz, Gunther, Heinz, Helmut, Helmuth, Jochen, Juergen.
German: from a vernacular form of the personal name   Paul  .
North German: metonymic occupational name for a pile driver, from Middle Low German pāl ‘pile, post, border marker’ or a topographic name for someone living close to one.

variants.name: McPhail
meanings: Scottish and Irish: from Gaelic Mac Phàil (Scottish) and Mac Phóil (Irish), patronymics from Gaelic forms of the personal name Paul . The most usual Irish Anglicization is McFall . Compare Faul 1.

variants.name: Pach
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Andrzej, Jozef, Pawel, Piotr.

Polish, Czech, Slovak, and Sorbian: from a pet form of a personal name beginning with Pa-, especially any of local equivalents of the name Paul and Slavic names like Polish and Sorbian Pakosław, Czech and Slovak Pačeslav (see Pac 1 and 2).
German: from a pet form of the personal name Bartholomäus (see Bartholomew ).

variants.name: Paavola
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Finnish Jouni, Osmo, Esko, Jouko, Sulo, Timo, Toivo, Waino.  Scandinavian Nels.

Finnish: habitational name from a farm so named, from the personal name Paavo, from Latin Paulus (see Paul ), + the locative ending -la. Both the farm name and the surname can be traced back to the 15th century. The surname occurs chiefly in western Finland.

variants.name: Pagel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Kurt, Gerhard, Otto, Ewald, Armin, Arno, Fritz, Hermann, Horst, Juergen.
North German: from the personal name Pagel, a Low German form of Paul .
North German (Pägel): metonymic occupational name for a calibrator, from Low German pegel ‘water level gauge, small container’ (see Pegler 3).

variants.name: Pal
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Sat, Ram, Dharam, Raj, Yash, Dilip, Ajay, Anil, Aruna, Chandra, Gopal, Indar.

Indian (West Bengal) and Bangladeshi: Kayastha name, from Sanskrit pāla ‘protector, keeper, guard, herdsman’ (compare gopāla ‘cowherd’), an epithet of the god Krishna and a common personal name. A Pala dynasty that was founded by a certain Gopala in the 8th century ruled in Bengal and Bihar until the 12th century.
Hungarian (Pál): from the personal name Pál, Hungarian form of Paul . This surname is also found in Slovakia (mainly spelled Pál) and especially in Romania.

variants.name: Pali
meanings: Hungarian (also Páli): patronymic from the personal name Pál, Hungarian equivalent of Paul .
Indian: unexplained.

variants.name: Palcic
meanings: Slovenian (Palčič) and Croatian (Palčić): from a diminutive of palec (Slovenian), palac (Croatian) ‘thumb’, used as a nickname for a physically small man; or perhaps a nickname from palčič (Slovenian), palčić (Croatian) ‘wren’, the bird being named with this word with reference to its small size.
Slovenian (Palčič): possibly also a patronymic from the personal name Pavel (see Paul ).

nameMeanings.name: Aline
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: In the Middle Ages this represented a contracted form of Adeline . In modern use it is either a revival of this or a respelling of Aileen . In Scotland and Ireland it has sometimes been chosen as representing an Anglicized spelling of the Gaelic vocabulary word àlainn (Scottish), álainn (Irish), meaning ‘lovely’.

en.lifeSummary: When Aline De St. Paul was born about 1354, her father, Stephen de Mallory, was 46 and her mother, Margaret Revell, was 36. She married William De Cheywynd II in 1364. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 3 daughters. She died about 1411, in Chetwynd, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 59.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Aline De St. Paul est née environ 1354, son père, Stephen de Mallory, avait 46 ans et sa mère, Margaret Revell, avait 36 ans. Elle a épousé William De Cheywynd II en 1364. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée environ 1411, à Chetwynd, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 59 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Aline De St. Paul um 1354 geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Stephen de Mallory 46 und ihre Mutter Margaret Revell 36. Sie hat 1364 William De Cheywynd II geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb um 1411 im Alter von 59 Jahren in Chetwynd, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Aline De St. Paul è nata il circa 1354, sua padre, Stephen de Mallory, aveva 46 anni e sua madre, Margaret Revell, aveva 36 anni. Lei sposato William De Cheywynd II nel 1364. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 3 figlie. Morì circa 1411, nel Chetwynd, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 59 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Aline De St. Paulが約1354年に生まれた時，父Stephen de Malloryは46歳，母Margaret Revellは36歳であった。1364年にWilliam De Cheywynd IIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は約1411年にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアチェトウインドで59歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Aline De St. Paul(이)가 대략 1354년에 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Stephen de Mallory(은)는 46세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Revell(은)는 36세였다. 그녀는 1364년 William De Cheywynd II(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1411년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 쳇w이드에서 59세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Aline De St. Paul родилась около 1354 г., её отцу по имени Stephen de Mallory было 46 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Revell, 36. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William De Cheywynd II в 1364 г. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она умерла около 1411 г. в местности четвинд, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 59 лет.

Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió aproximadamente 1411, en Chetwynd, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 59 años.
